short: The serving software that runs a model efficiently and exposes it through an API.
definition: An inference engine is the serving software that runs a trained model efficiently, handling batching, the KV cache (the model's running notes on the text), scheduling, and the request API. It turns the model's raw learned numbers into a service that many users can call at once with good speed and capacity. vLLM, SGLang, and TensorRT-LLM are widely used examples.
